description: Implement the Code Project IDE mode: new project type, file explorer, code editor, build pipeline, and project template. This is a single large feature covering all new files and the complete Code Project workflow.
steps: Step 1 - Create new branch 'feat/code-project-mode' from main

Step 2 - Create data model class pro.sketchware.codeproject.model.CodeProject.java that represents a code project with fields: sc_id, projectName, packageName, appName, versionCode, versionName, projectPath (on-device storage path). Include methods to get source directories (java, res, manifest). Store projects at /storage/emulated/0/.sketchware/mysc/list/<sc_id>/project with an extra field 'sc_project_type' set to 'code' in the HashMap to differentiate from block-based projects (existing ones use proj_type=1).

Step 3 - Create project template generator pro.sketchware.codeproject.template.CodeProjectTemplate.java that generates: AndroidManifest.xml (with package, activity declaration, basic permissions), MainActivity.java (Hello World with AppCompatActivity), activity_main.xml (simple LinearLayout with TextView), values/strings.xml, values/colors.xml, values/styles.xml (Theme.AppCompat based). Write files into the project's source directory under /storage/emulated/0/.sketchware/mysc/<sc_id>/app/src/main/.

Step 4 - Create file explorer adapter pro.sketchware.codeproject.ui.FileExplorerAdapter.java (RecyclerView adapter showing file tree with indent levels, folder expand/collapse, file icons for .java/.xml/other). Each item shows file name with folder/file icon. Tapping a file triggers a callback to open it in the editor. Tapping a folder expands/collapses its children.

Step 5 - Create layout file res/layout/activity_code_project.xml with: AppBarLayout (MaterialToolbar with project name, build button in menu), a DrawerLayout containing the file explorer (NavigationView or side panel) and main content area with the sora-editor CodeEditor widget. Include a bottom bar or toolbar with undo/redo/save buttons.

Step 6 - Create the main IDE activity pro.sketchware.codeproject.ui.CodeProjectActivity.java that: loads the project from sc_id intent extra, initializes the file explorer with the project's source tree, sets up sora-editor with setEditable(true) and appropriate language (Java/XML based on file extension) using EditorUtils patterns, handles file open/save/undo/redo actions, has a Build menu item that triggers compilation.
Step 7 - Create layout file res/layout/item_file_explorer.xml for file tree items (icon ImageView, name TextView, with left padding for indent).

Step 8 - Create the code project builder pro.sketchware.codeproject.build.CodeProjectBuilder.java that: takes the project's raw source files (not generated from blocks), runs AAPT2 to compile resources (reusing ResourceCompiler patterns from ProjectBuilder), runs ECJ to compile Java (reusing compileJavaCode pattern - source path is the project's java/ directory), runs D8 to dex the classes, builds the APK (reusing buildApk pattern), signs with debug key (reusing signDebugApk). This bypasses Jx/Lx/Ox/Ix generators entirely since source files already exist. It should create a yq-like path structure for bin/, gen/, compiled classes.

Step 9 - Create project creation activity pro.sketchware.codeproject.ui.CreateCodeProjectActivity.java with fields: App Name, Package Name, Project Name, and a Create button. On create: generate a new sc_id (using lC.b() pattern), save project metadata with sc_project_type=code, run CodeProjectTemplate to scaffold files, then open CodeProjectActivity.

Step 10 - Modify ProjectsFragment.java to add a way to create code projects. Add a dialog or menu option when the FAB is clicked that lets the user choose between 'Block Project' (existing flow via toProjectSettingsActivity()) and 'Code Project' (new flow via CreateCodeProjectActivity). Use a MaterialAlertDialogBuilder with two options.

Step 11 - Modify ProjectsAdapter.java to handle code projects in the list: when a code project is tapped (detected by sc_project_type=code in project HashMap), open CodeProjectActivity instead of DesignActivity.
Step 12 - Register all new activities in AndroidManifest.xml: CreateCodeProjectActivity and CodeProjectActivity.
Step 13 - Add string resources in res/values/strings.xml for: code_project, create_code_project, file_explorer, build_project, save_file, new_code_project, choose_project_type, block_project, etc.
Step 14 - Create menu resource res/menu/menu_code_project.xml with items: build (with play icon), save, undo, redo.
acceptance_criteria: The project compiles successfully with ./gradlew assembleDebug after all changes
New activities (CodeProjectActivity, CreateCodeProjectActivity) are properly registered in AndroidManifest.xml
ProjectsFragment FAB click shows choice between Block Project and Code Project
ProjectsAdapter differentiates code projects and routes to CodeProjectActivity
CodeProjectActivity uses sora-editor with setEditable(true) and configures Java/XML highlighting
CodeProjectBuilder compiles raw .java and .xml files using AAPT2+ECJ+D8+APK building, bypassing Jx/Lx/Ox/Ix
CodeProjectTemplate generates a valid Hello World Android project structure
File explorer shows project source tree with expand/collapse functionality
verification: Run: cd /projects/sandbox/Sketchware-Pro && ./gradlew assembleDebug to confirm the app builds with all new code
